skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: Objects P and Q each travel at a constant speed. The speed of object P is one-third the speed of object Q. Object P travels a distance of 6x inches in y seconds. Which expression represents the time, in seconds, it takes object Q to travel a distance of 36x inches?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Object P's speed is 6x/y inches per second, so object Q's speed is 3 × 6x/y = 18x/y inches per second. The time for Q to travel 36x inches is (36x) / (18x/y) = 2y seconds.

Choices B, C, and D result from forgetting the speed factor of 3 or inverting the relation.
